name: Grouper Saor
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces golden seedless raisins, soaked & drained
24 ounces grouper fillets, cut into 6-oz portions
virgin olive oil -- for sauteing the fish
recipeInstructions:
STEP ONE:
Soak golden raisins in water until they are plump, then drain.
STEP TWO:
Heat a saute pan on high heat until very hot. Add 4 ounces of oil and heat until smoking. Toss in onions and stir quickly so they don't burn. Season with salt and pepper and cook for 5 to 10 minutes until lightly caramelized. Add raisins and vinegars, and cook over low heat until all the vinegar is absorbed into the onions. Set aside, keeping warm.
STEP THREE:
Season the fish with salt, pepper, and oil. Grill each side for 2 to 3 minutes over high heat until cooked through. Place a bed of onions on the serving platter, top with fish, and garnish with pine nuts, chives, and additional olive oil.
